text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members regularly merge their code changes into a central shared repository. The key idea is that every time new code is committed, automated builds are triggered. Once the build is complete, the system automatically runs tests to verify that the new code works correctly with the existing codebase. This process is managed through a CI pipeline, which is essentially a series of steps that the code must pass. By doing this frequently, teams can catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. As a result, continuous integration helps ensure the software stays stable and reliable throughout development.
